'''Danny Halliwell''' (born 23 March 1981) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 2000s and 2010s. He played at club level in the [[Super League]] for [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] (two spells), the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], the [[Warrington Wolves]] ([[Loan (sports)|loan]]), the [[Leigh Centurions]] and the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]], and in the [[British rugby league system#History|Championships]] for the [[Keighley Cougars]] ([[Loan (sports)|loan]]), [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]], the [[Blackpool Panthers]], the [[Barrow Raiders]], the [[Oxford Cavaliers]] and the [[Swinton Lions]], and for the [[Leigh Miners Rangers]] ARLFC, as a {{rlp|wg|ce}}, or {{rlp|sr}}.<ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Danny Halliwell is the grandson of the rugby league {{rlp|pr}} who played the 1960s, and 1970s for [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]] (also coach 1978–1979), [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] and [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]]; [[Ken Halliwell (rugby league)|Ken Halliwell]].
